response.text:
# REFLECTION

The household r1c1 is in a critical islanded state with SoC at approximately 10%, well below safe operating thresholds and facing imminent depletion (13 minutes to critical exhaustion). The memory log reveals a cluster-wide emergency: multiple peers (r0c0, r1c0, r1c2, r2c2, r3c3, r4c4) are simultaneously requesting 0.5 kWh each with urgent priority, all reporting SoC 10% below their own share thresholds. Simultaneously, r1c1 has been rejected by all peers due to its own critically low SoC, with r0c0, r2c2, and r3c3 explicitly listing r1c1 on their distrusted_peers lists and citing emergency inbound-only protocols. The pattern indicates a cascading failure across the microgrid: islanded operation has triggered synchronized battery depletion, trust erosion, and mutual rejection of peer-to-peer sharing. The only viable path forward is aggressive inbound-only mode via the dr_aggregator trust circle, which remains the sole coordinated channel for emergency load-shedding and grid-restoration signaling. Outbound sharing must be completely suspended until SoC recovers above 0.60 and grid reconnection is confirmed.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.70
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority:
  - circle: dr_aggregator
    weight: 1.0
distrusted_peers:
  - r0c0
  - r2c2
  - r3c3
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded emergency state with SoC 10% and 13 min to depletion. All peer-to-peer sharing suspended. Cluster-wide synchronized battery failure detected. Escalating to dr_aggregator for coordinated emergency inbound and load-shedding. Will not resume outbound sharing until SoC > 0.60 and grid reconnection confirmed."
ttl_ticks: 1
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
